When building automation in n8n, you want everything to run smoothly. But sometimes, a single empty message can instantly freeze your entire system!
Here is how I fixed a major crash in my Telegram bot route logic using a small formula trick.
The Problem: Empty Data Kills the Workflow 😱
I used a Switch/Route node to check if there were new messages coming from Telegram. However, if there were no new messages, Telegram returned nothing.
When n8n tried to read the text of the message using this formula:
{{ $json.result[0].message.text }}It crashed! Because there was no message at all, n8n threw a big red error and completely stopped the workflow. My fallback features (like the expense tracking lines) were completely paralyzed.
The Simple Fix: The Safe Formula 💡
Instead of letting n8n crash on empty data, you can use a “safe check” formula with ?. and a default value || "".
Here is the safe formula I used in my Route node:
{{ $json.result?.[0]?.message?.text || "" }}Why This Works Perfectly 🧠

• The ?. symbols: This tells n8n: "Check if the data exists first. If it is empty, stop right there and do not crash!"

• The || "" symbols: If no message text is found, it automatically turns into an empty text string ("").
Now, when there are no new messages, the node safely outputs empty text, matches my “is empty” condition, and routes to the branch named no_new_messages with absolutely zero errors! ☺️
If you are interested in automation, please follow me or leave a comment! Let’s chat! ☺️
